arrivalSteps.title: Complete e-Arrival Card
arrivalSteps.description: India requires a mandatory online e-Arrival Card (arrive.gov.in) submitted within 72 hours before arrival. Fill in passport details, flight number, Indian address, and health declaration. Keep the confirmation reference handy.

arrivalSteps.title: Health Screening
arrivalSteps.description: Thermal screening is conducted at the entry point. Passengers arriving from countries flagged for specific disease alerts may be subject to additional checks or require a health certificate.

arrivalSteps.title: Immigration / Passport Control
arrivalSteps.description: Join the designated foreigner or e-Visa queue. Present your passport, e-Visa (or sticker visa), and e-Arrival Card confirmation. Officers will verify documents and may ask about intended activities and accommodation in India.

arrivalSteps.title: Biometrics Collection
arrivalSteps.description: All foreign nationals must provide fingerprint scans (all 10 fingers) and a facial photograph at the immigration counter. This is mandatory for visa holders including e-Visa entrants.

arrivalSteps.title: Baggage Claim
arrivalSteps.description: Proceed to the baggage hall and collect luggage from the assigned carousel. Retain baggage tags as they may be checked at the exit gate.

arrivalSteps.title: Customs Declaration
arrivalSteps.description: Declare currency/foreign exchange over USD 5,000 (or equivalent). Declare dutiable goods over INR 50,000 duty-free allowance. Prohibited items include satellite phones, certain drones, and narcotics. Use Red Channel to declare; Green Channel if nothing to declare.

fallbackAlerts.message: Avoid taxi touts outside the terminal. Use official pre-paid or app-based taxis (Uber/Ola) only.
Ensure your e-Visa is printed or accessible offline — it must be presented at immigration.

phrases.english: Where is the pre-paid taxi counter?

phrases.nativeScript: मेरे पास घोषणा करने के लिए कुछ नहीं है।
phrases.romanized: Mere paas ghoshna karne ke liye kuch nahi hai.

scenarios.description: Dress codes, shoe rules, and religious etiquette

arrivalSteps.title: Remove Shoes
arrivalSteps.description: Shoes must be removed before entering any place of worship. Shoe racks are at the entrance.

arrivalSteps.title: Cover Your Head at Gurdwaras
arrivalSteps.description: A head covering is mandatory in all Sikh Gurdwaras. Scarves are usually provided free at the entrance.

arrivalSteps.title: Modest Dress Required
arrivalSteps.description: Cover shoulders and legs. Sleeveless tops and shorts are not acceptable in most temples.

fallbackAlerts.message: Remove all footwear before entering any temple, mosque, or gurdwara — this is non-negotiable.
Leather items (belts, bags) may need to be removed or left outside at Hindu temples.
Photography is prohibited inside the inner sanctum of most Hindu temples.

phrases.english: Please show me where to remove my shoes.
phrases.nativeScript: कृपया मुझे दिखाइए कि जूते कहाँ उतारने हैं।
phrases.romanized: Kripaya mujhe dikhaaiye ki joote kahaan utarne hain.

phrases.english: Do I need a head covering?

scenarios.description: Meter rules, fare negotiation, and ride apps

arrivalSteps.title: Use Ride Apps
arrivalSteps.description: Uber and Ola are widely available and reliable. Always preferred over hailing a street cab.

arrivalSteps.title: Confirm Fare Before Boarding Auto
arrivalSteps.description: Auto-rickshaws may negotiate fares. Agree on a price before you board, or insist on the meter.

arrivalSteps.title: Cash is King
arrivalSteps.description: Keep small denomination notes (₹10–₹100) handy. Many drivers do not carry change for large notes.

fallbackAlerts.message: Insist on the meter for licensed taxis. Negotiate a fixed fare upfront for auto-rickshaws.
Use only app-based rides (Uber/Ola) after dark. Verify the driver's name and car plate before getting in.

phrases.english: How much to the city centre?
phrases.nativeScript: शहर के केंद्र तक कितना लगेगा?
phrases.romanized: Sheher ke kendra tak kitna lagega?

scenarios.description: Bargaining customs, scam awareness, and shopping etiquette

arrivalSteps.title: Bargaining is Expected
arrivalSteps.description: At street markets and bazaars, initial prices are inflated. Counter-offering at 40–60% of the asking price is normal.

fallbackAlerts.message: Bargaining is normal and expected in Indian markets. Start low and meet in the middle.
Always ask for a receipt for significant purchases, especially gems, textiles, and antiques.

phrases.nativeScript: आपकी सबसे अच्छी कीमत क्या है?
phrases.romanized: Aapki sabse acchi keemat kya hai?
